weekly_report.title: Weekly Model Landscape Report
weekly_report.summary: Claude Opus 4 dominates three categories in its debut week...

sections.heading: Key Changes This Week
sections.body:
Claude Opus 4 launched on May 5th and immediately claimed #1 in Text Chat (Chatbot Arena Elo 1392), Code Gen (SWE-bench Verified 64.3%), and Reasoning (ARC-AGI 64.3%). This is the first time a single model has topped three categories simultaneously.

OpenAI responded within 48 hours with a 40% API price cut on GPT-5, signaling that the competition is shifting from capability to economics.

sections.heading: Model Comparison: Claude Opus 4 vs GPT-5.5 vs Llama 4

models.name: Claude Opus 4
models.best_for: Complex multi-step reasoning, code generation, agent workflows

models.name: GPT-5.5
weaknesses: Reasoning trails Claude Opus 4 by 2.5 points on ARC-AGI
Code gen fell to #3 this week
models.best_for: Real-time applications, multimodal tasks, cost-sensitive deployments

models.name: Llama 4
models.best_for: Self-hosted deployments, privacy-sensitive applications, fine-tuning for specific domains

sections.heading: What's Driving Recent Progress
sections.body:
Three technical trends are behind this week's leaderboard shifts:

**1. Inference-time compute scaling.** Claude Opus 4's reasoning breakthrough comes from allocating more compute during inference rather than training. Dario Amodei described this as 'shifting from training-time scaling to inference-time scaling.' The model can 'think longer' on harder problems, trading latency for accuracy.

**2. Synthetic data and self-play.** Both Anthropic and OpenAI are now training on AI-generated data pipelines where models critique and improve their own outputs. This breaks the traditional ceiling of human-annotated training data.

**3. Tool-use integration at training time.** Claude Opus 4 was trained with tool-use as a first-class capability, not fine-tuned on top. This explains its strong performance on SWE-bench, which requires the model to navigate codebases, run tests, and iterate on solutions.

sections.heading: How to Choose: Decision Framework
sections.body:
**Pick Claude Opus 4 if:** You need the strongest reasoning or code generation, are building agent workflows, or prioritize accuracy over speed.

**Pick GPT-5.5 if:** You need fast inference, strong multimodal understanding, or are cost-sensitive after the 40% price cut.

**Pick Llama 4 if:** You need to self-host for privacy/compliance, want to fine-tune for a specific domain, or want to avoid vendor lock-in.

**Pick Gemini 2.5 Flash if:** You're optimizing for cost above all else. At $0.15/M tokens, it's 100x cheaper than Claude Opus 4 while maintaining competitive quality for simpler tasks.

sections.heading: Open Source Momentum
sections.body:
The open-source gap continues to narrow. DeepSeek V3 holds #2 in Code Gen with 58.9% on SWE-bench — only 5.4 points behind Claude Opus 4. In Image Gen, Flux 1.1 from Black Forest Labs has closed to within 35 Elo points of Midjourney v7.

The most interesting development is Wan 2.1 in Video Gen: its community-contributed LoRA adapters now outnumber Runway's official plugin marketplace, creating a grassroots ecosystem that closed-source providers struggle to match.

sections.heading: What to Watch Next Week
sections.body:
- Google I/O is expected to unveil Gemini 3, which could reshuffle the Reasoning and Multimodal categories
- Meta is rumored to release Llama 4 Scout, a smaller model optimized for edge deployment
- The first EU AI Act compliance deadlines for model providers take effect in Q3, potentially affecting which models are available in Europe
